projectDescription: Type-safe error handling for JavaScript & TypeScript using Result and ResultAsync types
keyFeatures: Type-safe error handling with Result<T, E> and ResultAsync<T, E>
Functional programming patterns with map, andThen, orElse
Safe wrapping of throwing functions with fromThrowable
Combining multiple Results with short-circuit or all-errors modes
Generator-based syntax with safeTry for ergonomic error propagation
publicApiSurface: Result.fromThrowable<Fn, E>(fn: Fn, errorFn?: (e: unknown) => E)
ResultAsync.fromPromise<T, E>(promise: Promise<T>, errorFn: (e: unknown) => E): ResultAsync<T, E>
ResultAsync.fromThrowable<A, R, E>(fn: (...args: A) => Promise<R>, errorFn?: (err: unknown) => E)
safeTry<T, E>(body: () => Generator<Err<never, E>, Result<T, E>>): Result<T, E>
fromThrowable<Fn, E>(fn: Fn, errorFn?: (e: unknown) => E)
fromAsyncThrowable<A, R, E>(fn: (...args: A) => Promise<R>, errorFn?: (err: unknown) => E)
fromPromise<T, E>(promise: Promise<T>, errorFn: (e: unknown) => E): ResultAsync<T, E>
Result.andThen<U, F>(fn: (value: T) => Result<U, F>): Result<U, E | F>
Result.asyncAndThen<U, F>(fn: (value: T) => ResultAsync<U, F>): ResultAsync<U, E | F>
Result.orElse<U, A>(fn: (error: E) => Result<U, A>): Result<U | T, A>
Result.match<A, B>(okFn: (value: T) => A, errFn: (error: E) => B): A | B
Result.andThrough<F>(fn: (value: T) => Result<unknown, F>): Result<T, E | F>
Result.asyncAndThrough<F>(fn: (value: T) => ResultAsync<unknown, F>): ResultAsync<T, E | F>
ResultAsync.map<A>(fn: (t: T) => A | Promise<A>): ResultAsync<A, E>
ResultAsync.mapErr<F>(fn: (error: E) => F | Promise<F>): ResultAsync<T, F>
ResultAsync.andThen<U, F>(fn: (value: T) => Result<U, F> | ResultAsync<U, F>): ResultAsync<U, E | F>
ResultAsync.orElse<U, A>(fn: (error: E) => Result<U, A> | ResultAsync<U, A>): ResultAsync<U | T, A>
ResultAsync.match<A, B>(okFn: (value: T) => A, errFn: (error: E) => B): Promise<A | B>
ResultAsync.andThrough<F>(fn: (value: T) => Result<unknown, F> | ResultAsync<unknown, F>): ResultAsync<T, E | F>
